title: Diffusion Models Are Real-Time Game Engines (GameNGen)
authors: Dani Valevski, Yaniv Leviathan, Moab Arar, Shlomo Fruchter
key_contribution: First neural game engine using two-phase paradigm: RL agent collects interaction data, diffusion model trained on frames+actions
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Direct technical precedent for NC_GUIWorld pipeline
conflict_evidence: Section 3: identical two-phase interaction-data-to-diffusion-model paradigm; formal definition equivalent to NC Eq. 2.1; action cross-attention conditioning equivalent to NC external injection mode

title: Diffusion for World Modeling: Visual Details Matter in Atari (DIAMOND)
authors: Eloi Alonso, Adam Jelley, Vincent Micheli et al.
key_contribution: Diffusion world model for RL (Atari); CS:GO interactive game engine extension using fixed human gameplay data
evidence_section: Section 3.1 (action via AdaGN), project page (CS:GO interactive engine with 87h human data)
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Direct paradigm precedent for NC_GUIWorld interactive simulation from human-collected data
conflict_evidence: Project page: 'diffusion model functions as an interactive neural game engine where users provide actions and the model autoregressively generates subsequent frames' — 87h human data, no RL, same paradigm as NC_GUIWorld

title: AVID: Adapting Video Diffusion Models to World Models
key_contribution: Lightweight adapter to adapt pretrained video diffusion models to action-conditioned world models without weight access; per-frame scale/shift conditioning from actions
evidence_section: Section 3.3 (adapter architecture, per-frame gamma/beta conditioning, masking mechanism)
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Design space precedent for NC_GUIWorld action injection into pretrained video diffusion model
conflict_evidence: Section 3.3: per-frame scale/shift from action embeddings via adaptive normalization — establishes the action injection design space NC_GUIWorld's injection ablation explores

title: Pre-Trained Video Generative Models as World Simulators (DWS)
authors: Zhanyi He, Xiaofan Zhang et al.
key_contribution: Per-block lightweight action conditioning (two linear layers, scale-shift) for converting pretrained video models into action-controlled world simulators; architecture-agnostic
evidence_section: Section 4.1 (two linear layers per block; x^i = x^i + FFN(LayerNorm(x^i)*(1+alpha^i)+beta^i))
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Direct precedent for NC_GUIWorld's internal injection mode (per-block cross-attention)
conflict_evidence: Section 4.1: per-block scale-shift modulation in transformer blocks — same design as NC_GUIWorld's internal injection mode but with two linear layers vs. cross-attention; precedes NC by 14 months

title: NeuralOS: Towards Simulating Operating Systems via Neural Generative Models
authors: Luke Rivard, Sun Sun, Hongyu Guo, Wenhu Chen, Yuntian Deng
key_contribution: Ubuntu XFCE GUI simulation via hierarchical RNN (O(1) per-timestep state) + diffusion renderer; 256-frame memory retention; same domain and data source as NC_GUIWorld
evidence_section: Section 3 (RNN architecture, Gaussian cursor encoding), Appendix P (differentiation from game engines), Appendix B (limitations)
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Direct competitor in same domain (Ubuntu XFCE, Anthropic CUA data); cited but grossly under-discussed
conflict_evidence: Appendix P: RNN state enables recall of interactions far in the past; explicitly solves OS long-horizon state persistence problem NC_GUIWorld leaves as future work (CFR-004); Section 1: same Ubuntu XFCE GUI simulation goal from mouse/keyboard inputs

title: Genie: Generative Interactive Environments
authors: Jake Bruce, Michael Dennis, Ashley Edwards et al. (Google DeepMind)
key_contribution: 11B foundation world model from internet videos with latent action model; action-controllable virtual world generation
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Cited ancestor; conceptual context for generative interactive environments
conflict_evidence: N/A — cited in NC paper; less direct than GameNGen/DIAMOND

title: Computer-Using World Model (CUWM)
authors: Yiming Guan, Rui Yu, John Zhang et al.
key_contribution: Desktop GUI world model (Microsoft Office); text-then-visual factorization; RL refined; postdates NC paper
evidence_section: N/A — abstract only; postdates NC paper (April 2026)
conflict_evidence: Cannot verify without full paper — postdates NC paper

title: Vid2World: Crafting Video Diffusion Models to Interactive World Models
key_contribution: Causal masking on temporal attention + action-per-frame conditioning via lightweight MLP to convert video diffusion to interactive world model
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Methodologically parallel to NC_GUIWorld; may postdate NC submission

title: ViMo: A Generative Visual GUI World Model for App Agents
key_contribution: Visual GUI world model for mobile app agents; Symbolic Text Representation for text in image patches
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Mobile (not desktop) GUI world model — adjacent domain
conflict_evidence: Cannot verify without full paper; mobile vs. desktop distinction limits direct overlap

title: Latent Action Pretraining from Videos (LAPA)
key_contribution: Unsupervised VLA pretraining without ground-truth action labels using VQ-VAE-based latent actions between frames
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Adjacent method (unsupervised action learning vs. NC's explicit action conditioning)

title: iVideoGPT: Interactive VideoGPTs are Scalable World Models
authors: Jialong Wu, Shaofeng Yin et al.
key_contribution: Autoregressive transformer integrating visual observations, actions, and rewards into token sequence for world modeling
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Adjacent method (autoregressive vs. diffusion); game/robot focus

title: Neural Turing Machines
authors: Alex Graves, Greg Wayne, Ivo Danihelka
key_contribution: Differentiable external memory for neural networks; NTM lineage
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Foundational contrast — cited by NC paper as what NCs are NOT
conflict_evidence: Cited correctly; NC paper's differentiation from NTM/DNC is accurate

title: Hybrid computing using a neural network with dynamic external memory (DNC)
authors: Alex Graves, Greg Wayne et al.
key_contribution: Dynamic memory allocation and temporal linking for neural networks; DNC extends NTM
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Foundational contrast — cited by NC paper
conflict_evidence: Cited correctly; NC paper's differentiation from NTM/DNC is accurate

title: World Models
key_contribution: World models as dream environments for RL agents; separates world model from controller
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Foundational contrast — cited by NC paper
conflict_evidence: Cited correctly; NC paper's distinction from world models (NC is the computer; world model predicts for agent) is coherent

title: DreamerV3: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models
authors: Danijar Hafner, Jurgis Pasukonis, Jimmy Ba, Timothy Lillicrap
key_contribution: RSSM latent dynamics world model for multi-domain RL; state-of-the-art world model for agents
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Foundational contrast — cited by NC paper
conflict_evidence: Cited correctly as canonical world model for RL

title: ControlNet: Adding Conditional Control to Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
authors: Lvmin Zhang, Anyi Rao, Maneesh Agrawala
key_contribution: Zero-initialized residual branch for conditional control of diffusion models
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Used but possibly uncited — NC_GUIWorld residual injection mode described as 'ControlNet-style'
conflict_evidence: Cannot verify without full paper; citation gap is minor

title: AdaWorld: Learning Adaptable World Models with Latent Actions
key_contribution: Pretraining world model with self-supervised latent actions; robotics/game focus

title: MineWorld: A Real-Time and Open-Source Interactive World Model on Minecraft
key_contribution: Visual-action autoregressive Transformer for real-time Minecraft interaction
relation_to_evaluated_paper: Adjacent domain (game vs. OS); real-time video from actions
